Matthew Yglesias (@mattyglesias)

A strong case from @MattBruenig that “normal” tax and transfer policies are adequate to make AI-driven productivity growth win-win — unfortunately the voters do not seem to want to hear this. 

theargumentmag.com/p/ai-coul…

Joe Hagan (@SnoobularTubes)

Irony isn’t popular on this site tho. Nor is reading the article before piping off. I get it. Reading is hard!



— https://nitter.net/SnoobularTubes/status/2034441264951681255#m

kind of underdiscussed how carney's most consequential policy move by far is cutting immigration

The reason these guys are so aggressive and vicious is that on some level, they know they're the bad guys.

They lost the moral high ground a long time ago, and they try to make up for it by flinging as much shit as they can.




Lucas C. De Hart (@Dehartspeaks1)

Don’t worry we’ll get to you soon enough fascist Islamophobe



— https://nitter.net/Dehartspeaks1/status/2034314440783622145#m

This is why everyone in Star Trek is either a frontier explorer or a very rich unemployed person


François Chollet (@fchollet)

Current AI is a librarian of existing knowledge. Science requires an explorer of the unknown. You don't win a Nobel Prize by staying in the library.



— https://nitter.net/fchollet/status/2034676840896635257#m

It's ok to date someone who used to be your student.

Hell, I did a whole podcast with someone who used to be my student.


Popstonox (@Popstonox)

Daniel Biss, candidate for IL-09 in tomorrow's election, has been accused of having an inappropriate relationship with a student during his tenure as a professor at the University of Chicago

Megan Wachspress, now a Stanford law lecturer, says she was that student




Community note: Per Wachspress’s own allegations, Biss was only 5 or 6 years older

Trump sucks


(((Harry Enten))) (@ForecasterEnten)

This is the most troublesome sign I've seen for Trump & the GOP.

Trump's now a record low 41 pts underwater on the cost of living per Yahoo/YouGov.

He's 60 pts underwater on the issue with indies!!

Wave adios to the House & maybe Senate cause you can't win with these numbers.

Video
  





— https://nitter.net/ForecasterEnten/status/2034650699275935769#m
It’s kind of weird that the day after the Irish drinking holiday isn’t the one called Boxing Day.

It was only a few years ago that I learned that some people are very insistent that you spell it “St. Paddy’s Day” and not “St. Patty’s Day” and I was so excited to have something new to be pedantic about.

Somehow, it was decided that 5th graders at my kid’s school should wear their class t-shirts today which are GRAY. 

Admittedly, my town is more Italian Catholic than Irish Catholic, but, like, every other little league coach is named Michael Somethingirish. So I can’t be the only one grumbling.
￼Finally threw out my Christmas tree!

If a character dynamic doesn’t work for you that’s fine. But that may actually mean this particular piece of entertainment isn’t right for you, not that you are morally or intellectually superior to it.

What’s that? You think Dwight is more sympathetic than Jim? You understand that everyone who made the show wanted you to feel the other way but you’re a crazy maverick who manages to feel the opposite? 

Sounds like you’re more interested in patting yourself on the back than enjoying a TV show.

Hey, here’s some unsolicited advice: Usually the best way to enjoy and engage with a piece of entertainment is not to try to outsmart it. 

Not liking the main character who is supposed to be sympathetic does not mean you have DEFEATED the work of art, nor should that be your goal.

I think not enough attention was payed to the passing of the last living Little Rascal this year. 
www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie...

Oh sure. When I was a kid, Perfect Strangers, Who’s the Boss, Growing Pains, Mr. Belvedere, Just the 10 of Us … all appointment viewing. But even then I knew they weren’t as good as Cheers or Newhart or early seasons of the Cosby Show (I didn’t know! I was 8!)

Would we count Wonder Years? That was a little before Roseanne and it was ABC’s last Emmy for Best Comedy until Modern Family two decades later. 

But, yes, ABC was pretty dedicated to pleasant, middle-of-the-road family fare like Who’s the Boss and Growing Pains.

For quite a while, but for some reason their US advertisements at the moment are all about why you don’t need to be afraid to shop there even though they’re weird and different. Which they are not. www.instagram.com/reel/DTbRDUZ...

I know we’re living in a undiscovered circle of hell at the moment, but this morning at Aldi I found a shopping cart that already had a quarter in it so after my shopping was done, guess who was walking out with 50 cents in his pocket!

I would like to remind everyone that in the 90s the Surgeon General suggested that it might be okay to tell children that masturbation exists and the country threw a tantrum so big she had to resign and we just didn’t have a Surgeon General for over three years.
This is definitely the repressed Irishman in me talking, but my worst nightmare would be hearing someone say that I was displaying “big feelings.”
Heck, I quoted “Her Majesty” in my Best Man toast at my brother’s wedding! (to be fair, it was kind of a joke reference because I had just quoted “The End” — you gotta love track-order humor)

On Valentine’s Day, the Wordle solution was BLOOM but apparently, if Wordlebot had it narrowed down to two options, it would have guessed GLOOM instead. 

Did Wordlebot recently go through a breakup?

On Valentine’s Day, the Wordle solution was BLOOM but apparently, if Wordlebot had it narrowed down to two options, it would have guessed GLOOM instead. 

Did Wordlebot recently go through a breakup?
Ooh! I’m late to the table on this, but have people been making jokes about “the arrest formerly known as Prince”?
Save us, Digital Cronkite!. Social media tore our society apart. Perhaps AI can put it back together.
Roundup #79: The revenge of macroeconomics. Ehrlich; AI and knowledge; The Strait of Hormuz; Government debt and inflation; Japanese robots; Democrats and taxes; Smartphones
The future isn't what it used to be
San Francisco's urban revival is in danger. If the Board of Supervisors falls to the Progressives, the old dysfunction will return.
Are we in the foothills of World War 3?. The coalitions are hardening, and the weapons are being deployed.
Something feels weird about this economy
If AI is a weapon, why don't we regulate it like one?. Thoughts on the fight between Anthropic and the Department of War.
Japan can be America's arsenal (by Rie Yano - Coral Capital)
Superintelligence is already here, today. It's going to revolutionize science. It also might take control of this planet.
The shape of the multipolar world is a little clearer
Roundup #78: Roboliberalism. AI and productivity; Yuppie Fishtank Theory; Global development; American living standards; George Borjas
Why does America feel worse than other countries? Crime.. In most ways, the U.S. is a typical rich country. Except for crime.
The Citrini post is just a scary bedtime story. AI might take your job, but it probably won't crash the economy -- and if it does, we know how to deal with it.
Does anyone know why we're still doing tariffs?. The ridiculous policy has taken on a life of its own.
Democratic economic policy in the age of AI. The economy is changing fast. Democrats need to be a rock in the storm.
